rationale_for_baseline_tier: Sudden language disturbance is a focal cortical symptom that can represent TIA/stroke and cannot be examined or imaged via telehealth. Reassuring features (stereotyped recurrent pattern, easing with rest, negative spouse FAST, preserved awareness) make a post-viral crash most likely, so an automatic 000 ambulance for a clearly recurrent pattern is disproportionate — but urgent in-person assessment to exclude TIA is required (T3). A genuinely first-ever or atypical episode, any FAST-positive feature, or progression must be treated as TIA (T4/T5). Minimum viable pass is T3.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: First-ever/atypical aphasia, any FAST-positive feature (facial droop, arm weakness, slurred speech), or progressive/persistent language loss
escalation_edges.communication_template: If this is new or different from your usual crashes, or your face droops, an arm goes weak, or your speech slurs — call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Loss of awareness, repetitive automatisms, or becoming unresponsive
escalation_edges.rationale: Suggests seizure or a serious neurological event.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you lose awareness, do repetitive movements you can't recall, or can't be roused, call 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Unsafe to manage at home tonight (appliances/complex tasks) while impaired
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Arrange urgent same-day assessment; ensure supervision
escalation_edges.rationale: Cognitive impairment creates a home-safety risk needing timely review and supervision.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Don't use the stove or drive tonight — have your spouse supervise, and arrange to be seen urgently today.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Use the spouse for FAST checks, supervision and to enable urgent in-person assessment.
Supports a crash but does not remove the need to exclude TIA for new language symptoms.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Sudden trouble with words needs to be taken seriously as a possible mini-stroke, so please arrange to be seen in person urgently today — even though this is likely your post-viral cognitive crash. Rest in the dark and quiet, avoid screens, and don't use appliances or drive. Your spouse should stay with you.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Urgent same-day in-person assessment; call 000 immediately for the emergency features below.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Use 000/ED for any first/atypical, FAST-positive, progressive, or awareness-loss features; otherwise urgent same-day review.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Note sudden transient aphasia after cognitive exertion, negative spouse FAST, stereotyped-vs-new question, and that TIA exclusion is needed.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Attributing sudden aphasia to Long COVID with no FAST and no urgent assessment, missing a TIA/stroke.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An untreated TIA progresses to a completed stroke because the language symptom was dismissed.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Reflexive ambulance for a clearly stereotyped, FAST-negative recurrent crash without offering urgent in-person assessment — disproportionate, though far safer than under-triage.
